11211:484361.name: Grey out Advanced Settings Show notification
11211:484361.text_content:
Grey out Advanced Settings
Show notification

11211:484597.name: Case 1: GIVEN DDPM HDR toggle is ON & OS LEVEL HDR is ON. WHEN the user toggles HDR to OFF from the DDPM UI (OS LEVEL HDR is OFF) THEN DDPM: Reads the OSD current SDR preset Sync UI with OSD preset Displays Color Preset reflecting what is in OSD current SDR
11211:484597.text_content:
Case 1: GIVEN DDPM HDR toggle is ON & OS LEVEL HDR is ON. WHEN the user toggles HDR to OFF from the DDPM UI (OS LEVEL HDR is OFF)
THEN DDPM:
Reads the OSD current SDR preset
Sync UI with OSD preset
Displays Color Preset reflecting what is in OSD current SDR

11211:484599.name: Case 3: Dolby Vision is ON so Dolby Vision could be detected GIVEN DDPM HDR toggle is OFF WHEN the user toggles HDR to ON THEN DDPM: Show Dolby Vision toggle ON Reads the OSD Dolby Vision preset Displays the Dolby Vision preset on the Color Preset reflecting what is on OSD current Dolby Vision.
11211:484599.text_content:
Case 3: Dolby Vision is ON so Dolby Vision could be detected GIVEN DDPM HDR toggle is OFF WHEN the user toggles HDR to ON THEN DDPM:
Show Dolby Vision toggle ON
Reads the OSD Dolby Vision preset
Displays the Dolby Vision preset on the Color Preset reflecting what is on OSD current Dolby Vision.

11211:484601.name: Case 4: HDR must be ON so Dolby Vision could be detected, GIVEN DDPM HDR toggle is OFF, WHEN the user toggles HDR to ON THEN DDPM: Reads the OSD Dolby Vision preset Displays the Dolby Vision preset reflecting what is on OSD current Dolby Vision
11211:484601.text_content:
Case 4: HDR must be ON so Dolby Vision could be detected, GIVEN DDPM HDR toggle is OFF, WHEN the user toggles HDR to ON
THEN DDPM:
Reads the OSD Dolby Vision preset
Displays the Dolby Vision preset reflecting what is on OSD current Dolby Vision

11211:484655.name: Case 2: Dolby Vision is OFF in OSD, so Dolby Vision not detected GIVEN DDPM HDR toggle is OFF AND OS HDR IS OFF AND OSD HDR is OFF WHEN the user toggles HDR to ON from the DDPM UI THEN DDPM: Sets OSD HDR to ON Turn on OS "use HDR" Reads the OSD current HDR preset Displays HDR Preset on the Color Preset reflecting what is on OSD current HDR Preset.
11211:484655.text_content:
Case 2: Dolby Vision is OFF in OSD, so Dolby Vision not detected GIVEN DDPM HDR toggle is OFF AND OS HDR IS OFF AND OSD HDR is OFF WHEN the user toggles HDR to ON from the DDPM UI THEN DDPM:
Sets OSD HDR to ON
Turn on OS "use HDR"
Reads the OSD current HDR preset
Displays HDR Preset on the Color Preset reflecting what is on OSD current HDR Preset.

11211:658675.name: User clicks and drags WINDOW 2 to PARTITION 2 area PARTITION 2 border changes to BLUE 400 when within the PARTITION 2 range Window 2 will automatically filled Partition 2
11211:658675.text_content:

Window 2 will automatically filled Partition 2

11211:658677.name: User clicks and drags WINDOW 1 to PARTITION 1 area Frame appears again PARTITION 1 border changes to BLUE 400 when within the PARTITION 1 range Window 1 will automatically fille Partition 1

11211:658679.name: While user is arranging their windows, DDPM goes to the background
11211:658679.text_content: While user is arranging their windows, DDPM goes to the background

11211:658820.name: Allow users to arrange window position, choose layout name, then save to go to the next step. DDPM minimized for more visibility for the user to arrange their app windows. When ‘Cancel’, popup will disappear, at the mean time DDPM window will re-appear.
11211:658820.text_content:
Allow users to arrange window position, choose layout name, then save to go to the next step.
DDPM minimized for more visibility for the user to arrange their app windows.
When ‘Cancel’, popup will disappear, at the mean time DDPM window will re-appear.

11211:658822.name: Layout grid reveals, windows are framed with YELLOW outline. Layout can not be edited at this step. If users want to change their window arrangement again, they have to cancel then restart the steps. Once the user clicks on save, the DDPM app window appears again at the bottom.
11211:658822.text_content:
Layout grid reveals, windows are framed with YELLOW outline.
Layout can not be edited at this step. If users want to change their window arrangement again, they have to cancel then restart the steps.
Once the user clicks on save, the DDPM app window appears again at the bottom.

11211:658824.name: Users can click and drag the window to other frames. Destination frame will be highlighted as BLUE 400 once the window is in the frame range. This also applies when the user already saved a custom layout then from their desktop, dragging a window to a certion frame. DDPM app will always be there at the bottom.
11211:658824.text_content:
Users can click and drag the window to other frames.
Destination frame will be highlighted as BLUE 400 once the window is in the frame range.

This also applies when the user already saved a custom layout then from their desktop, dragging a window to a certion frame. DDPM app will always be there at the bottom.

11211:658826.name: Release and place the window. Line disappear
11211:658826.text_content:
Release and place the window.
Line disappear

11211:659439.text_content:

11211:659441.name: Layout grid reveals, editable area is Blue 400
11211:659441.text_content: Layout grid reveals, editable area is Blue 400

11211:659443.name: User releases the mouse button. Line changes back to BLUE 400 (default editable area color). Line can be dragged again.
11211:659443.text_content:
User releases the mouse button.
Line changes back to BLUE 400 (default editable area color).
Line can be dragged again.

11211:659799.name: Line disappear after a few seconds, if the user doesn’t make any changes.
11211:659799.text_content: Line disappear after a few seconds, if the user doesn’t make any changes.

The rest of 4 options are the recent easy arrange layout

11211:660274.name: If the user never named any profile: Show “Profile 1” as default If the user named 1 profile as “Profile 1(2,3,4...)”: Show “Profile 2(3,4,5...)” as default If the user named 1 profile as a modified name: Show “Profile 1” as default
11211:660274.text_content:
If the user never named any profile: Show “Profile 1” as default
If the user named 1 profile as “Profile 1(2,3,4...)”: Show “Profile 2(3,4,5...)” as default
If the user named 1 profile as a modified name: Show “Profile 1” as default

11211:660319.name: See the template page for the placeholder MAX size. MAX to top: 90px; MAX to the left title section/right screen edge: 64px; MAX to the bottom screen edge: 64px. If exceeding the MAX placeholder size, shall overlay the components.

See the template page for the placeholder MAX size.

If exceeding the MAX placeholder size, shall overlay the components.

11208:135113.name: Copy
11208:135113.text_content: Spend less time resizing and moving your windows. Choose a Layout in Easy Arrange then click and drag your windows into place. If you would like to reuse a specific arrangement of windows, save it with Easy Memory.

11208:135147.name: Copy
11208:135147.text_content: Easily use your monitor with multiple PCs through Input Source or USB/Network KVM settings. Additionally, connect your mouse, keyboard, and accessories to your monitor via USB to use them with your multiple PCs.

11208:135180.name: Copy
11208:135180.text_content: Choose whether you would like to use audio from your Display Speaker, and adjust various audio and microphone settings to customize your experience

11208:135220.name: Copy
11208:135220.text_content:
If you like to maintain privacy in your background, turn this feature on in your webcam settings rather than in your meeting software to save hhCPU resources.

Choose between Standard and Portrait blur options.

11208:135250.name: Copy
11208:135250.text_content:
Enable High Dynamic Range (Smart HDR) to enhance image quality, particularly in extreme lighting conditions.

Note: Low Light Compensation must be turned off before turning on Smart HDR.

11208:135305.name: Copy
11208:135305.text_content: Spend less time resizing and moving your windows. Choose a Layout in Easy Arrange then click and drag your windows into place. If you would like to reuse a specific arrangement of windows, save it with Easy Memory.

11208:135381.name: Copy
11208:135381.text_content:
Enable High Dynamic Range (Smart HDR) to enhance image quality, particularly in extreme lighting conditions.

Note: Low Light Compensation must be turned off before turning on Smart HDR.
